title: 17. Main Chat Remote
description: LAN-first phone remote setup for the real main chat runtime: local bridge, backend process, Expo app, pairing, and test order.

steps.title: Open Main Chat Remote
steps.body: Main Chat Remote is separate from Multi Persona Roleplay. It bridges the real main chat runtime to the phone app through a small local bridge and a separate LAN backend.

steps.title: Start the Local Bridge
steps.body: Local bridge exposes safe runtime status and chat actions to the remote backend. Keep this on only while testing the phone remote.

steps.title: Create the Backend Venv Once
steps.body: Create Backend Venv prepares the separate remote backend environment. This should live outside the main NC runtime so phone networking does not become part of the core chat pipeline.

steps.title: Start the LAN Backend
steps.body: Start LAN Backend launches the phone-facing process. Test on the same LAN first with numeric pairing before considering any internet relay or tunnel layer.

steps.title: Use the Phone Command Text
steps.body: The command box shows the current Expo/phone startup hints and bridge status. For real-device testing, start the backend first, then run the Expo app and pair from the Android device on the same network.
